senses.definition: A family of chemical compounds derived from benzophenone, typically used as a photoinitiator or UV absorber in various products.
examples: Commercial sunscreens often contain benzophenones to filter UV light.
The cosmetic formulation included several benzophenones as fragrance fixatives.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic chemical compound containing benzene fused to a thiazine ring, used as a core structure in some pharmaceutical or dye molecules.
examples: The drug candidate features a benzophenothiazine core.
Researchers studied benzophenothiazine derivatives for bioactivity.

senses.definition: A polycyclic heteroaromatic compound where a benzene ring is fused with an oxazine-containing unit, used in advanced materials or dye chemistry.
examples: Benzophenoxazine derivatives were explored as dyes.
The study synthesized a series of benzophenoxazines for optoelectronic applications.

senses.definition: A benzene-based trihydroxy compound (phloroglucinol derivative) in which a benzene ring is substituted to form a specialized benzenetriol.
examples: Benzophloroglucinol can participate in multi-step syntheses.
The intermediate contained a benzophloroglucinol unit.

senses.definition: Relating to a chemical group containing phosphorus attached to a benzene ring.
examples: The compound was analyzed for its benzophosphinic substituent.
Benzophosphinic derivatives showed interesting reactivity.

senses.definition: A fused bicyclic heteroaromatic compound consisting of benzene and phthalazine rings.
examples: Benzophthalazine derivatives are studied in medicinal chemistry.
The scaffold used in the library included benzophthalazine.

senses.definition: A benzene-derived ketone linked to a pinacone motif, used as a chemical scaffold in research.
examples: The team synthesized a benzopinacone-based library.
Benzopinacone derivatives exhibited chromophoric properties.

senses.definition: A benzene ring fused to a pyran ring; a common structure in many natural products and dyes.
examples: Many natural pigments contain a benzopyran core.
The benzopyran scaffold is common in flavonoids.

senses.definition: Relating to a benzopyran ring system or a benzopyranyl group in chemical structures.
examples: The benzopyranyl moiety was introduced to modify solubility.
A benzopyranyl fragment appeared in the synthetic intermediate.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic compound containing a pyrazolone ring fused to a benzene ring.
examples: Benzopyrazolone derivatives have been explored for pharmaceutical activity.
The synthesis targeted a benzopyrazolone core.

senses.definition: A polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon with multiple fused benzene rings; a known environmental carcinogen.
examples: Benzo[a]pyrene is a well-known benzopyrene used in carcinogenic studies.
Exposure to benzopyrene-containing soot is a health concern.

senses.definition: Multiple benzopyrene molecules or related polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
examples: Samples contained several benzopyrenes detected by spectroscopy.
Regulators monitor benzopyrenes due to their toxicity.

senses.definition: A positively charged benzopyrylium cation, a reactive intermediate in organic synthesis.
examples: The mechanism involved benzopyrylium as an intermediate.
Stabilized benzopyrylium ions were generated under specific conditions.

senses.definition: A fused bicyclic heteroaromatic compound consisting of benzene and quinoline rings.
examples: Benzoquinoline derivatives are studied for electronic properties.
The scaffold combines a benzene ring with a quinoline system.

senses.definition: A six-member ring with two carbonyl groups; a simple benzoquinone used as a chemical reagent and in redox chemistry.
examples: Benzoquinone acts as an electron acceptor in redox reactions.
The laboratory prepared benzoquinone for the oxidation step.

senses.definition: Multiple benzoquinone molecules or related quinone compounds.
examples: The reaction yielded several benzoquinones.
Benzoquinones are used as oxidizing agents in some syntheses.

senses.definition: A fused heterocycle composed of benzene and quinoxaline rings.
examples: Benzoquinoxaline scaffolds are investigated for bioactivity.
The compound features a benzoquinoxaline core.

senses.definition: A sulfonamide-derived benzo- heterocycle used in synthetic chemistry or pharmacology.
examples: Benzo sulfimide derivatives were evaluated for activity.
The core scaffold resembles a sulfonamide-bearing benzene ring.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of benzosulfimide; a sulfonimide-containing benzene ring compound.
examples: Benzo sulphimide derivatives were synthesized.
The two spellings refer to the same class of compounds.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic compound containing a tetrazine ring fused to a benzene ring.
examples: Benzotetrazine derivatives are explored for energetic materials.
The benzotetrazine core is seen in some dye systems.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic compound with a tetrazole ring fused to a benzene ring.
examples: Benzotetrazole derivatives are used in energetic formulations.
The benzotetrazole scaffold is common in click chemistry reagents.

senses.definition: A heterocycle consisting of a benzene ring fused to a thiazine ring.
examples: Benzothiazine derivatives are studied for pharmacological activity.
The scaffold includes a benzothiazine moiety.

senses.definition: A benzene ring fused to a thiazole ring; common in dyes, pesticides, and pharmaceuticals.
examples: Benzothiazole is a building block in some dyes.
Several benzothiazole derivatives showed antimicrobial activity.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic compound with a benzene ring fused to a thiazoline ring.
examples: Researchers investigated benzothiazoline as a potential ligand.
The benzothiazoline framework supported catalysis.

senses.definition: A fused heterocycle containing sulfur in a diazole-like ring fused to a benzene ring.
examples: The study focused on benzothiodiazole derivatives for material science.
Benzothiodiazole scaffolds can contribute to electronic properties.
